topic: AI Coding CLI Tools Comparison 2026: Claude Code vs Codex vs Gemini CLI vs Junie

competitors.title: Claude Code vs Codex CLI vs Gemini CLI (2026 Comparison) - DeployHQ
key_points: Claude Code: TypeScript, proprietary, 200K–1M context (Opus 4.6), no free tier, $20/mo Pro; best for multi-file editing and codebase understanding
Codex CLI: Rust + TypeScript, Apache 2.0, uses GPT-5.3/o3/codex-mini-latest, no free tier (needs ChatGPT sub or API key); best for CI/CD integration with native GitHub Actions support
Gemini CLI: TypeScript, Apache 2.0, 1M context, had free tier (Gemini Flash only since March 2026); best for Google Cloud workflows and large context codebases
All three support MCP, custom system instructions (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, GEMINI.md), and sandboxed execution
Real-world tests: Claude Code most thorough (~90s), Codex CLI fastest (~45s), Gemini CLI most current via Google Search (~60s)

competitors.title: Claude Code vs Codex vs Gemini CLI: Feature Comparison | IntuitionLabs
key_points: Claude Code built on Claude Opus 4.x with SWE-Bench Verified score of 80.8–80.9%
Codex CLI scores 77.3% on Terminal-Bench 2.0 vs Claude Code's 65.4% on same benchmark
Claude Code: 115,000 developers, 195M lines of code processed weekly; annualized revenue run-rate exceeded $1B
Claude Code mentioned in 75% of coding-agent social media discussions vs Codex 22% and Gemini CLI 3%
Enterprise case studies: Stripe 10K-line Scala→Java migration in 4 days via Claude Code; Ramp 80% faster incident response

competitors.title: Which AI Coding Tools Do Developers Actually Use at Work? | JetBrains Research Blog
key_points: JetBrains January 2026 survey: 10,000+ developers worldwide in 8 languages
11% of developers use JetBrains AI Assistant and/or Junie; 9% use AI Assistant regularly, 5% use Junie
Claude Code: 28% primary tool selection; Cursor: 24%; GitHub Copilot remains top among enterprise developers
57% of developers had heard of Claude Code in January 2026 (vs 31% in April–June 2025); 18% use it at work (6x increase)
59% of developers run three or more AI coding tools in parallel for different tasks
In January 2026, 90% of developers regularly used at least one AI tool at work

competitors.title: Junie CLI, the LLM-agnostic coding agent, is now in Beta | JetBrains
key_points: Junie CLI launched in beta March 2026 as a standalone terminal agent (previously IDE-only plugin)
LLM-agnostic: supports models from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and Grok; lets you choose based on cost vs quality
BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) pricing or JetBrains AI licenses ($100/year AI Pro, $300/year AI Ultimate)
Supports MCP one-click server installation with automatic detection and recommendation
Works in terminal, any IDE, CI/CD pipelines, GitHub, and GitLab; available on Linux, macOS, Windows
Uses JetBrains static analysis for semantic code understanding — not just text, but symbols, dependencies, and type checking

competitors.title: Claude Code vs Codex CLI vs Gemini CLI: Benchmarked & Ranked | CodeAnt
key_points: Codex CLI has highest overall benchmark score (67.7%) and strongest backend performance (58.5%); Junie ranks second overall at 63.5%
Claude Code produces most thorough multi-file configurations with lowest hallucination rate
Gemini CLI added Plan Mode (March 2026): read-only planning phase before writing any code, addresses most common AI agent failure mode
Junie CLI allows model flexibility — use Gemini 3 Flash for cheaper tasks, Claude Sonnet for quality work
Claude Code's smaller context window compensated through intelligent indexing and parallel sub-agents

stats.fact: Claude Code: 115,000 developers and 195M lines of code processed weekly within 4 months of mid-2025 launch; annualized revenue run-rate exceeded $1B
Claude Code: 80.8–80.9% SWE-Bench Verified score, highest among CLI coding tools
Codex CLI: 77.3% Terminal-Bench 2.0 score; 67.7% highest overall benchmark; Apache 2.0 license
Gemini CLI: 1M token context window (3–4M characters); free tier now limited to Gemini Flash models only as of March 25, 2026
Junie: 11% of developers worldwide use JetBrains AI Assistant/Junie; 5% specifically use Junie (JetBrains Jan 2026 survey of 10K+ devs)
Claude Code: 28% primary tool selection; Cursor: 24% — together account for 52% of primary tool choices
90% of developers regularly used at least one AI tool at work in January 2026; 59% run 3+ tools in parallel
stats.source: JetBrains AI Pulse survey Jan 2026 / Digital Applied survey

stats.fact: Codex CLI April 2026 pricing update: codex-mini-latest priced at $1.50/1M input tokens, $6/1M output tokens, 75% prompt caching discount
stats.source: OpenAI Codex pricing page / UI Bakery

stats.fact: Junie CLI benchmarks: 63.5% overall score (second best); uses Gemini 3 Flash/GPT-4.1 for summarization by default
Stripe used Claude Code for 10K-line Scala→Java migration in 4 days (vs ~10 engineer-weeks manually); deployed to 1,370 engineers
Developers spend 11.4 hours/week reviewing AI-generated code vs 9.8 hours writing new code — a reversal of the 2024 pattern

angles: LLM-agnostic vs model-locked: Junie CLI's multi-provider flexibility (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Grok) vs Claude Code's single-model bet — which is the better long-term strategy?
Terminal-first vs IDE-native: Junie's evolution from IDE plugin to CLI agent vs Claude Code/Codex/Gemini born as pure terminal tools
Plan Mode wars: Gemini CLI's March 2026 read-only planning phase vs Claude Code's ask/approve flow vs Junie's propose-then-execute
Benchmark trust gap: SWE-bench Verified vs Terminal-Bench vs real-world tasks — why lab scores and developer trust diverge
BYOK flexibility: Junie's bring-your-own-key model vs subscription pricing (Claude Code, Codex) — total cost analysis for teams
The CI/CD dimension: Which tool truly belongs in your deployment pipeline — Codex CLI's native GitHub Actions vs Junie CLI's GitLab/GitHub support vs Claude Code hooks
Context window reality: Does Gemini CLI's 1M token window matter more than Claude Code's parallel sub-agents for real codebases?
Open source vs closed: Codex CLI (Apache 2.0) + Gemini CLI (Apache 2.0) + Junie (partial open-source) vs Claude Code (proprietary)
keywords: Claude Code vs Codex vs Gemini CLI vs Junie
